name: Jade Pratt
focus: Computational imaging • Atmospheric turbulence correction • Optical instrumentation
bio: Jade A. Pratt is a Ph.D. student in Physics at Northern Arizona University, working under Dr. Keith Nowicki in the Computational Imaging and Optical Physics Laboratory. He serves as both a Graduate Research Assistant and Graduate Teaching Assistant, with a background in mathematics and a focus on optical instrumentation. His research investigates the impact of atmospheric turbulence on ground-based imaging systems, focusing on how these distortions can be characterized and computationally corrected. By analyzing how distortions appear across both image and spatial-frequency domains, his work seeks to develop computational methods that complement or reduce reliance on traditional hardware-based solutions. His broader goal is to enable more accessible, lower-cost approaches to high-resolution imaging for remote sensing applications.
